mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 6.5% triggers a labor market downturn that suppresses corporate earnings growth, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE above 6.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to capture at least 50% of variance in S&P 500 forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production below its moving average triggers a demand shock that reduces corporate earnings growth, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO below its moving average are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to capture at least 30% of variance in S&P 500 forward returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in housing starts above its moving average signals a supply-side expansion that boosts corporate earnings growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with HOUST above its moving average are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to capture at least 40% of variance in S&P 500 forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate below its moving average triggers a monetary easing cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS below its moving average are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to capture at least 50% of variance in S&P 500 forward returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in payroll employment above its moving average signals a labor market upturn that boosts corporate earnings growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with PAYEMS above its moving average are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to capture at least 30% of variance in S&P 500 forward returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in real GDP below its moving average triggers a demand shock that reduces corporate earnings growth, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 below its moving average are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to capture at least 40% of variance in S&P 500 forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in industrial production above its moving average signals a supply-side expansion that boosts corporate earnings growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO above its moving average are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to capture at least 50% of variance in S&P 500 forward returns over any 2-year rolling window.
